domain: Ainu inau prayer stick (Hokkaido indigenous; whorls of curled wood shavings; tier-count = magnitude; shaving direction = which kamuy)
specific_mechanism: Magnitude encoding via discrete repetition of identical atomic instruction at prompt level; tier-count K∈{1..5} acts as priority signal in place of adjective amplitude.
llm_application: AINU-INAU-TIER-STACK: a prompt magnitude encoding scheme where the SAME atomic instruction sentence is tier-stacked K times at the start of the system prompt. K is the priority level (1=mild, 5=critical) and REPLACES adjective-amplitude (e.g., 'very strongly please' → 5× identical 'please' instructions). Decoder treats tier-count as magnitude. Differs from Leviathan 2512.14982 (which uses K=2 fixed) by (a) tier-K generalisation K∈{1..5}, (b) explicit calibration of K against priority levels, (c) per-instruction K (different instructions in same prompt can have different K). Recombines Leviathan prompt-repetition + priority-tier vocabulary from Lakera prompt-engineering guide.
novelty_claim: Leviathan 2512.14982 establishes prompt-repetition-at-K=2 as accuracy booster. AINU-INAU-TIER-STACK extends to K∈{1..5} with explicit per-instruction priority calibration. Small extension of existing technique; not a wholly new mechanism. Expected FAIL with strong functional overlap.
